Woman's Role as Homemaker

President Belle S. Spafford

(Address delivered at the afternoon session of the Annual General Relief Society Conference, held in the Tabernacle, September 29, 1949)

THE subject upon which I shall speak this afternoon, namely, "Woman's Role as Homemaker" is not new; it has been brought before you times without number, both in general conferences and regular meetings of the society. It is an old subject but one which remains with us as ever important.

President David O. McKay, in addressing a Relief Society conference a few years ago, made this statement: "Although woman's life is filled with almost everything that is good and lovely, it is not difficult to choose the two activities that are paramount in her world." These he defined as homemaking and compassionate service. Unfortunately, this viewpoint with regard to homemaking is not shared by all women.
